meta.name: Edinburgh Postnatal Depression Scale
meta.description: The EPDS is a 10-item self-report scale designed to screen for postnatal depression. It focuses on the cognitive and affective features of depression rather than somatic symptoms, making it particularly suitable for the postpartum period. It can also be used during pregnancy and in non-postnatal populations.
meta.author: Cox, Holden & Sagovsky (1987)
meta.reference: Cox JL, Holden JM, Sagovsky R. Detection of postnatal depression: Development of the 10-item Edinburgh Postnatal Depression Scale. Br J Psychiatry. 1987;150:782-786.
meta.instruction: As you are pregnant or have recently had a baby, we would like to know how you are feeling. Please select the answer that comes closest to how you have felt in the past 7 days, not just how you feel today.

questions.text: I have been able to laugh and see the funny side of things

choices.label: As much as I always could

questions.text: I have looked forward with enjoyment to things

choices.label: As much as I ever did
Rather less than I used to
Definitely less than I used to

questions.text: I have blamed myself unnecessarily when things went wrong
I have been anxious or worried for no good reason
I have felt scared or panicky for no very good reason
Things have been getting on top of me

choices.label: Yes, most of the time I haven't been able to cope at all
Yes, sometimes I haven't been coping as well as usual
No, most of the time I have coped quite well
No, I have been coping as well as ever

questions.text: I have been so unhappy that I have had difficulty sleeping
I have felt sad or miserable
I have been so unhappy that I have been crying
The thought of harming myself has occurred to me

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your score is within the normal range. No significant depressive symptoms detected.
suggestions: Continue taking care of yourself and your baby
Maintain social connections and support networks
Rest when possible and accept help from others

ranges.label: Possible Depression
ranges.description: Your score suggests possible depression. Monitoring and further evaluation may be beneficial.
suggestions: Talk to your healthcare provider about how you are feeling
Reach out to family or friends for support
Ensure you are getting adequate rest
Consider repeating this screening in 2-4 weeks

ranges.label: Fairly High Possibility of Depression
ranges.description: Your score indicates a fairly high possibility of depression. Professional evaluation is recommended.
suggestions: Consult your healthcare provider or a mental health professional
Postnatal depression is treatable and common; seeking help is a sign of strength
Maintain daily routines as much as possible
Accept help with baby care to ensure you have time for self-care

ranges.label: Probable Depression
ranges.description: Your score suggests probable depression. Please seek professional help promptly.
suggestions: Seek professional help from your healthcare provider as soon as possible
If you have thoughts of harming yourself or your baby, please call a crisis hotline immediately (e.g., 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline)
Do not feel ashamed; postnatal depression is a medical condition, not a personal failing
Effective treatments including therapy and medication are available
